evals.prompt: I have a scikit-learn fraud detection model that's performing well in our test environment. I need to deploy it as a real-time API that our payment service calls for every transaction. We process about 1000 transactions per second. What's the best approach?
evals.expected_output: Should design a serving architecture considering the 1000 TPS requirement, recommend appropriate serving pattern, address preprocessing consistency, and set up monitoring with drift detection.
assertions: Considers the 1000 TPS requirement in architecture choice
Recommends containerized serving with horizontal scaling
Sets up input validation for the prediction endpoint
Designs drift detection (fraud patterns change over time)
Plans a safe rollout strategy (shadow mode or canary)
Includes latency monitoring (fraud checks must be fast)

evals.prompt: Our recommendation model has been in production for 6 months and click-through rates are declining. The data science team says the model metrics look fine on their test set. What's going on and how do we fix it?
evals.expected_output: Should identify this as likely data drift or concept drift, recommend checking production data distributions against training baseline, and suggest monitoring and retraining infrastructure.
assertions: Identifies data/concept drift as the likely cause
Explains why test set metrics can look fine while production degrades
Recommends comparing production feature distributions against training baseline
Suggests PSI or KS tests for drift detection
Recommends setting up continuous monitoring to prevent recurrence
Suggests a retraining pipeline with fresh data
Does NOT just say 'retrain the model' without diagnosing the cause
